stats_fallback.delayed_11_months: Original delivery slot was August 2022; Virgin pushed her commercial debut back by 11 months. Fincantieri formally delivered her on 7 December 2022.
stats_fallback.christened: No traditional christening. Virgin Voyages does not hold godmother ceremonies. Maiden voyage event was a celebratory launch in Piraeus, not a christening.
stats_fallback.cabins_note: 1,408 cabins with 86% balcony, 93% oceanview. European king beds, Peek-a-Boo showers, Yellow Leaf hammocks on balconies.
stats_fallback.length: 912 ft (277.03 m per VesselFinder / 278 m nominal per Fincantieri)
stats_fallback.builder: Fincantieri S.p.A., Sestri Ponente yard (Genoa, Italy) — hull yard number 6289
stats_fallback.adults_only_note: Strictly 18+. No children's clubs, no kids' menus, no family staterooms, no waterslides.

related.class_notes: Lady Class. Resilient Lady is the THIRD of Virgin Voyages' four Lady Class ships, all built by Fincantieri at Sestri Ponente. Lead ship Scarlet Lady (yard 6287, delivered 14 February 2020), Valiant Lady (yard 6288, delivered 1 July 2021), and Brilliant Lady (yard 6304, entered commercial service 5 September 2025 after an 18-month lay-up). All four share the Fincantieri Mille platform with identical 110,000 GT / 2,770 guests / 1,160 crew / 1,408 cabins specs. Resilient Lady's distinction is her deployment history — she was Virgin's first ship to sail Greek Isles (14 May 2023), Virgin's first ship in Australia and New Zealand (Sydney Harbour 4 December 2023), and Virgin's first trans-Indian-Ocean repositioning voyage. She is hardware-identical to her sisters — the only per-ship venue exclusives are Scarlet's Lucky Lotus Chinese concept and Valiant's Ariya Indian restaurant (from May 2026).

deployment.current_2026_04_11: Returning to PortMiami (Terminal V), ETA 07:00 on 11 April 2026 for same-day Saturday turnaround. Current rotation: 7-night Western Caribbean + Bimini Beach Club round-trips from PortMiami. April 2026 departures: 4, 11, 18, 25.
deployment.deployment_correction: Resilient Lady is NOT an Australia/Asia-Pacific ship as of 2026. Previous framing was stale from her one Australia season (Dec 2023 – Mar 2024). Virgin Voyages cancelled the 2024-25 Australia programme on 26 February 2024 citing Red Sea / Suez Canal security (Houthi attacks) that made the long repositioning voyage unviable.
deployment_history: May–October 2023: Inaugural Greek Isles season from Piraeus (Athens)
11 December 2023 – 27 March 2024: Australia / New Zealand from Melbourne (one season only; Sydney Harbour arrival 4 December 2023)
May–October 2024: Second Mediterranean season from Piraeus
Winter 2024-25: San Juan, Puerto Rico — 7/8/10/11-night Caribbean round-trips
Winter 2025-26 / Spring 2026: PortMiami — 7-night Western Caribbean + Bimini Beach Club (current)

history.delivered: 7 December 2022 (after 11-month delay — Virgin parked the completed hull at Palermo through autumn 2022 while pushing her commercial debut back)
history.2023_12_04_sydney_arrival: 4 December 2023 — arrived Sydney Harbour with Sir Richard Branson in attendance; first Virgin Voyages ship in Australia
history.2023_12_11_melbourne_season_start: 11 December 2023 — Australia / New Zealand season begins from Melbourne
history.2024_02_26_australia_cancellation: 26 February 2024 — Virgin Voyages cancels planned 2024-25 Australian programme citing Red Sea / Suez Canal security concerns (Houthi attacks)
history.2024_03_27_melbourne_season_end: 27 March 2024 — Australia / New Zealand season ends
history.2024_05_mediterranean_return: May 2024 — returns to Mediterranean service ex-Piraeus
history.2024_winter_san_juan: Winter 2024-25 — repositioned to San Juan, Puerto Rico for 7/8/10/11-night Caribbean round-trips
history.2025_winter_portmiami: Winter 2025-26 / Spring 2026 — repositioned to PortMiami for 7-night Western Caribbean + Bimini Beach Club round-trips

discrepancies_flagged.imo_contamination_check: Verified IMO 9805348 for Resilient Lady against VesselFinder, MarineTraffic, CruiseMapper, and Wikipedia (Equasis-sourced). Cross-checked against Scarlet (9804801), Valiant (9805336), and Brilliant (9870654) — all four sister IMOs are distinct. NOTE: Valiant 9805336 vs Resilient 9805348 differ by only 12 — this is the highest cross-contamination risk pair in the class.
discrepancies_flagged.deployment_correction_australia: Previous page implied Resilient Lady is an Australia-based ship. STALE since 26 February 2024, when Virgin cancelled the 2024-25 Australia programme due to Red Sea / Suez Canal security concerns. As of April 2026 she is a Caribbean ship from PortMiami (with a history of Greek Isles 2023 → Australia Dec 2023–Mar 2024 → Mediterranean 2024 → San Juan winter 2024-25 → PortMiami winter 2025-26).
discrepancies_flagged.class_name_wrong: Previous stats JSON said 'Virgin Class'. Fixed to 'Lady Class'.
discrepancies_flagged.title_leak: Previous page had 'Resilient Lady — Virgin Voyages | In the Wake' as ship name. Fixed.
discrepancies_flagged.delivery_year_wrong: Previous page said delivered 2020 or 2022 inconsistently. Correct: formally delivered 7 December 2022, after an 11-month delay from her original August 2022 slot.
discrepancies_flagged.main_dining_room_hallucination: Previous FAQ said Resilient Lady offers 'the main dining room and buffet'. Virgin has neither.
discrepancies_flagged.wrong_dining_hero_image: Removed Cordelia Empress Food Court image.
discrepancies_flagged.no_godmother_invention: Explicitly states no godmother. Maiden voyage from Piraeus was a celebratory launch, NOT a christening.
discrepancies_flagged.no_cirque: Virgin Voyages has never had Cirque du Soleil.
